sections.paragraph:
- Original: Σιδών

- Transliteration: Sidon

- Phonetic: sid-one'

- Definition: Sidon or Zidon = "hunting"

1. an ancient and wealthy city of Phoenicia, on the east coast of the Mediterranean Sea, less than 20 miles (30 km) north of Tyre

- Origin: of hebrew origin H6721

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Location

- Strong's: Of hebrew origin [H6721]; Sidon (that is Tsidon) a place in Palestine: - Sidon.
